---
title: "Microbiome Taxa by Body Site"
---

This section complements the workflow pages by showing the dominant microbes that commonly appear at different human body sites. Each body-site page uses an interactive taxonomy navigator so you can move from **phylum** to **class**, **order**, **family**, **genus**, and representative **species**.

For now, the site includes the **intestinal microbiome**, with colors anchored to the major gut phyla highlighted throughout the tree:

- **Actinomycetota** — purple
- **Bacteroidota** — blue
- **Bacillota** — green
- **Pseudomonadota** — red

The goal is to make it easy to connect high-level ecological patterns ("Bacteroidota-rich community") to the lower-level taxa you often see in profiling tables or papers.

## Available body-site pages

::: {.microbiome-site-grid}
::: {.microbiome-site-card}
### [Intestinal microbiome](intestinal.qmd)

Explore an interactive phylogenetic tree of representative gut taxa, including major clades within Actinomycetota, Bacteroidota, Bacillota, and Pseudomonadota.
:::
:::

As additional body-site pages are added, this section can expand to oral, skin, vaginal, and respiratory microbiomes while keeping the same navigation pattern and color logic.
